title: Hansel and Gretel
lines: Near a great forest there lived a poor woodcutter with his two children, Hansel and Gretel.
Their mother had died, and their father had married a woman who was very cruel to the children.
Times were hard, and there was not enough food for everyone.
One night the stepmother said to the woodcutter, 'Tomorrow we must take the children into the forest and leave them there.'
Hansel heard this plan and went outside to fill his pockets with white pebbles that shone in the moonlight.
The next morning, the parents took the children into the forest.
Hansel secretly dropped the white pebbles along the path.
When they were left in the forest, Hansel told Gretel not to be afraid.
Following the pebbles that shone in the moonlight, they made their way home.
The woodcutter was glad to see them, but the stepmother was angry.
A few days later, she insisted again that the children be taken into the forest.
This time Hansel could not get pebbles, so he used breadcrumbs instead.
But the birds ate the breadcrumbs, and the children became lost in the forest.
They walked for many hours until they came to a house made entirely of gingerbread and cake.
Inside lived an old witch who loved to eat children.
She locked Hansel in a cage and made Gretel her servant.
Every day she asked Hansel to put out his finger so she could feel how fat he was getting.
But Hansel was clever and put out a thin chicken bone instead.
The impatient witch soon decided to cook him anyway.
She asked Gretel to check if the oven was hot enough.
Gretel pretended she did not understand and asked the witch to show her.
When the witch bent down to look into the oven, Gretel gave her a push and shut the door.
The wicked witch was locked inside, and Gretel freed her brother.
In the house they found a great treasure of pearls and precious stones.
They filled their pockets and made their way through the forest.
After walking for a long time, they came to a great lake.
There was no bridge, but a white swan offered to carry them across.
When they reached the other side, the forest began to look familiar.
Soon they saw their father's cottage and ran toward it with joy.
The woodcutter had been very sad since the children disappeared.
He wept with happiness to see them again.
The stepmother had died while they were gone.
The children showed their father the treasure they had found, and they never wanted for anything again.
